posts.author: Arjan (Arjan)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello,

I’m trying to use AI to fill a date (deadline) column.
I have asked AI to fill this column with the last date from a timeline column.

When I finished my request, Monday made two automations.
One automation works, but the otherone fails and is also wrong, cause I don’t want he do this when a item is created.

What do I have to do in my AI request where he don’t make this automation?
When I remove the AI from the column, he remove both automations.

image1778×232 25.5 KB

posts.author: Clover_Gal (Desiree McCarthy | monday.com Ambassador)
posts.cookedHtml:
What about using an AI block in a workflow instead of at the column level?

Desiree - www.thecleverclovers.com

posts.author: Arjan (Arjan)
posts.cookedHtml:
Good Morning Desiree,

Thanks for your reply.
What I did now is building a workflow and used the build workflow with AI functionality.
That’s what you mean with AI block right?

After finishing the workflow, it works the way I want it

I red that there will be credited AI credits by using AI.
Does this also counts for what I did now or is that free of use?

Kind regards,
Arjan

posts.author: Clover_Gal (Desiree McCarthy | monday.com Ambassador)
posts.cookedHtml: Glad it worked! Here’s the monday article about AI credits for you - https://support.monday.com/hc/en-us/articles/29544502265746-Changes-to-AI-Credits-model
